_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 TX state legislators: terry-wilson (HD-20 R), dade-phelan (HD-21 R, former Speaker, RETIRING), judith-zaffirini (SD-21 D, pro-life-identity/pro-choice-votes), brian-birdwell (SD-22 R, RETIRING), christian-manuel (HD-22 D, openly gay). Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; no party inference.

profile.background: Retired U.S. Army Colonel; HD-20 (Williamson Co.) since 2017; chairs House Higher Education Committee. Confirmed running 2026 (filed R primary 2026-03-03).

terry-wilson@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: Establishment-conservative Army-veteran Republican. Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 100% pro-life. Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA school-choice vouchers. Self-claimed record: anti-CRT, $5B property-tax cut, fully funding law enforcement, increased border-security (Operation Lone Star) funding. NRA-affiliated but no specific letter grade located. No cross-party deviation found. Individual roll calls on SB8/HB1927 not machine-verified this pass; scored cells rest on TAL rating, the confirmed SB2 voucher vote, and his own campaign statements.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 100%; campaign 'Earned 100% Pro-Life Voting Record.'

1.note: 100% TAL across sessions incl. votes for TX trigger ban (near-total post-Dobbs prohibition) as a House Republican.

2.note: No public position on IVF/embryo/stem-cell found.

3.note: No public position on euthanasia/assisted suicide found.

4.note: No campaign-finance check performed; no abortion-industry PAC money documented either way.

0.note: No statement/vote on marriage definition found.

1.note: No vote/statement on same-sex marriage/civil unions found.

2.note: Campaign credits 'protecting children from radical gender modification' — self-stated rejection of minor gender interventions.

3.note: No position on divorce reform found.

4.note: Anti-CRT statement is racial, not a documented stance on LGBTQ promotion in schools.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA/school-choice; tabled the Talarico anti-voucher amendment.

1.note: No vote/statement on parental medical/gender consent found.

0.note: Baptist membership is background, not a documented policy position.

3.note: No SB1515/school-prayer vote located for him.

0.note: No CBDC position found.

2.note: Property-tax-cut record does not map to balanced-budget amendment cell; no evidence.

0.note: No paper-ballot position found.

1.note: Generic 'election integrity reform' claim too vague to pin photo-ID/citizenship.

3.note: SB1 coauthorship not confirmed; generic claim insufficient.

0.note: Campaign: 'Fought to fully fund law enforcement' — affirmative pro-police advocacy.
No 10th-Amendment/nullification vote found.
Campaign: 'Voted to increase funding for border security' (Operation Lone Star: border barrier + National Guard presence).
NRA-affiliated but HB1927 individual vote not verified; no specific rating.

profile.background: Former Speaker of the Texas House (Jan 2021 - Jan 2025); HD-21 (Beaumont/SE Texas). Real-estate developer. RETIRING: announced 2025-08-21 he will not seek reelection in 2026; serves out term through Jan 2027.

dade-phelan@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CANDIDACY: announced 2025-08-21 NOT seeking reelection (retiring lame duck). As Speaker (2021-24) shepherded major conservative bills (SB8 Heartbeat Act, HB1927 permitless carry, SB1 2021 election law, Operation Lone Star, SB4 border). Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 100%. KEY INTRA-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) led the 2023 Paxton impeachment — got him censured by the Texas GOP; (2) his House blocked school vouchers in 2023, and in 2025 he was one of only TWO Republicans to vote NO on SB2 vouchers (scored FALSE on school choice); (3) SB1515 Ten Commandments bill died in his House (characterization, left unscored as no recorded Phelan vote). Won a brutal 2024 runoff vs David Covey before retiring. As Speaker he rarely cast floor votes; many positions are agenda/gatekeeping acts.

0.note: TAL 2025 = 100%; as Speaker passed SB8 Heartbeat Act, framed 'sanctity of life' as a priority.

1.note: His House passed SB8 and the trigger ban (near-total post-Dobbs prohibition); TAL 100%.

0.note: No marriage-definition statement found.

1.note: 2019 'done bashing on the gay community' quote is about rhetoric, not marriage law; no clean vote.

2.note: As Speaker passed SB14 (minor gender-transition ban) and stated support: 'we will not stand for minors being influenced to make life-altering decisions.'

4.note: 2019 conciliatory LGBTQ remark cuts against; no clean schools-policy vote.

0.note: DEVIATION: one of only two Republicans to vote NO on SB2 (2025) school-choice vouchers; only R to back a ballot-referendum amendment. His House also blocked vouchers in 2023.

1.note: No clean parental-consent vote (SB14 scored under marriage).

2.note: No CRT/SOGI position specific to Phelan found.

3.note: HB1181 passed under his speakership but no Phelan-specific vote.

0.note: No evidence.

3.note: SB1515 Ten Commandments died in his House per news characterization, but no recorded Phelan vote to score.

0.note: No CBDC position found.

2.note: Property-tax relief does not map to balanced-budget cell.

0.note: No paper-ballot position found.

1.note: SB1 strengthened mail-ballot ID matching but not new photo-ID/citizenship; not pinned.

3.note: As Speaker shepherded SB1 (2021) restricting mail-in voting, drive-thru/24hr voting, and ballot harvesting; signed warrants for quorum-breaking Democrats.

4.note: SB1 did not include a Zuckerbucks ban (separate later bill).

0.note: No anti-defund/pro-police vote found (Paxton impeachment is intra-party, not a police cell).
No 10th-Amendment/nullification vote found.

2.note: SB4 immigration enforcement scored under border.

3.note: Speaker-procedural acts not scored as personal sovereignty statements.

0.note: Operation Lone Star (~$6.5B incl. state border wall) passed under his speakership; defended SB4 as 'strongest border security law in the nation.'

2.note: 2023 SB4 is a state illegal-entry crime, not a sanctuary ban; his 2017 SB4 vote not confirmed.

0.note: As Speaker, HB1927 permitless/constitutional carry (2021) passed the House under his leadership; described as a solid supporter.

profile.religion: Catholic (lector, Blessed Sacrament Catholic Church, Laredo)
profile.background: Dean of the Texas Senate (longest-serving member); SD-21 (Laredo) since 1987; first Hispanic woman in the TX Senate. Confirmed running 2026 (won 2026-03-03 Dem primary; faces R Julie Dahlberg).

judith-zaffirini@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. HIGH-VALUE CROSS-PRESSURE CASE: self-identifies as personally pro-life Catholic ('I will always be pro-life') BUT votes consistently pro-choice — voted against HB2 (2013), in the Democratic NO bloc on SB8/HB1280 (2021), said abortion is 'the law of the land' and women must have 'access to safe and legal abortions,' held a 100% NARAL rating. Life[0] left NULL to honor that genuine personal/policy contradiction; life[1] FALSE on her documented votes against restrictions. Generally votes the Democratic line (against SB14, SB2 vouchers, permitless carry HB1927 [floor statement against], SB4 border). NOTABLE DEVIATION (D for conservative position): voted FOR SB11 school-prayer bill (2025), one of only three Democrats — scored TRUE on christian_liberty prayer cell. John Birch Freedom Index lifetime 24%.

0.note: Genuine contradiction: self-IDs 'always pro-life' (Catholic) yet says abortion is 'the law of the land' and women need 'safe and legal' access; mixed — left null.

1.note: Voted AGAINST HB2 (2013); in the Democratic NO bloc on SB8 Heartbeat Act and the trigger ban (2021). 100% NARAL rating.

4.note: NARAL rating reflects votes, not money; no PAC-funding finance data pulled.

0.note: No marriage-definition vote found.

2.note: Voted against SB6 (2017 'bathroom bill') and against SB14 (2023 minor gender-transition ban) — does not reject transgender policy.

4.note: Pattern of votes against SB6/SB14 indicates she does not oppose LGBTQ-identity policy.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB2 (2025) school-choice/ESA vouchers with the Senate Democratic caucus.
No evidence.

3.note: DEVIATION: voted FOR SB11 (2025) school-prayer bill, one of only three Democrats (with Royce West, Juan Hinojosa).

0.note: Freedom Index per-bill mark on HB1056 constitutional money is unverified direction; not scored.
No paper-ballot position found.

3.note: Senate Democratic caucus opposed SB1 (2021), the marquee party-line election-integrity bill restricting mail-in voting/drop boxes/harvesting; no crossover.

0.note: No anti-defund/pro-police vote found.
No 10th-Amendment vote found.
No border-barrier position found.

2.note: In the Senate Democratic bloc voting NO on SB4 (2023) state immigration-enforcement/anti-sanctuary law (party-line 17-11); border Democrat, no crossover.

0.note: Spoke against HB1927 (2021 permitless carry) on the Senate floor: it 'would merely make it easier to carry a firearm legally in public, including by persons who should not'; consistent with NO vote.

profile.religion: Christian (committed to Christ age 10; founded Face the Fire Ministries)
profile.background: Retired U.S. Army Lt. Col.; 9/11 Pentagon survivor (burned over 60% of body); SD-22 (Granbury) since 2010. RETIRING: announced 2025-06-30 he will not seek reelection in 2026; serves out term. Rep. David Cook running to succeed him.

brian-birdwell@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CANDIDACY: announced 2025-06-30 NOT seeking reelection (retiring). Consistent across-the-board conservative with a deep authored-bill record. Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 100%; Texas Values 2025 = 94% 'Faith & Family Champion'; YCT career ~82. Joint author SB8 Heartbeat Act; SB14 gender-transition ban (Senate 31-0); cosponsor SB10 Ten Commandments + SB11 prayer + SB965 Coach Kennedy; coauthor SB2 ESA vouchers + SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights; coauthor SB1 (2021) election integrity + author SJR37 citizenship-voting amendment; author SB405 LEO protections + coauthor SB6 bail reform; author SB29 anti-COVID-mandate; coauthor SB4 (2017) sanctuary ban; HB1927 permitless carry + author of school-marshal/church-carry bills. No cross-party deviation found. NRA/TSRA 'A'+endorsement on record but most recent located is 2012-dated.

0.note: TAL 2025 = 100%; cosponsor SB33 'No Taxpayer Funds for Abortion'; 'unyielding... to protect innocent human lives.'

1.note: Joint author of SB8 Texas Heartbeat Act; coauthor SB4 (2021) restricting abortion-inducing medication; supported the trigger ban.

0.note: No marriage-definition vote found.

2.note: YEA on SB14 (2023) minor gender-transition ban (Senate 31-0); Texas Values credits HB229 'What is a Woman Act' and SB1257 detransitioners bill.

4.note: Texas Values credits support for SB18 'Drag Story Hour' restrictions.

0.note: Coauthor of SB2 (2025) ESA/school-choice; endorsed by Texas Home School Coalition.

1.note: Supported SB12 (2025) Parents' Bill of Rights.

0.note: Supported SB965 (2025) 'Coach Joe Kennedy Law' protecting the right to pray in public employment/schools.

1.note: Clergy-protection bills (SB24/SJR27) are not professional conscience-exemption cells; not scored here.

3.note: Cosponsor of SB10 (2025) Ten Commandments in classrooms and SB11 (2025) prayer in schools.

0.note: No CBDC position found.

2.note: Property-tax/homestead record does not map to balanced-budget cell.

0.note: No paper-ballot position found.

1.note: Joint author SJR1 (2017) voter ID; author SJR37 (2025) constitutional amendment clarifying non-citizens cannot vote.

3.note: Coauthor SB1 (2021) election integrity / ballot-harvesting restrictions.

0.note: Author SB405 (2019) protecting law enforcement from false accusations; coauthor SB22 rural sheriff/prosecutor funding.
No 10th-Amendment/nullification bill found.

1.note: Author SB29 (2023) prohibiting government COVID-19 mask/vaccine/closure mandates.

2.note: No separate guns/education overreach measure isolated.

0.note: No specific border-barrier/military authorship pinned.

2.note: Coauthor SB4 (2017) sanctuary-city ban; coauthor SB4 (2021) human-smuggling penalties.

0.note: Voted for HB1927 (2021 permitless carry, Senate party-line 17-13); author SB741/SB870 school-marshal carry, coauthor SB535 church carry.

profile.background: Legally Christian Manuel Hayes; HD-22 (Beaumont/Port Arthur) since 2023; former 18-yr staffer to predecessor Joe Deshotel; Vice Chair, House Human Services. Openly gay. Confirmed running 2026 (advanced from 2026-03-03 Dem primary).

christian-manuel@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Conventional progressive Democrat (John Birch Freedom Index lifetime 17%). Openly gay; LGBTQ Victory Fund 'Spotlight' candidate ('committed to killing bills that attack marginalized communities'); spoke on the House floor AGAINST SB14 defending gender-affirming care for minors — scored FALSE across biblical_marriage and on school choice (voted for the anti-voucher budget amendment). NOTE for transparency: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 52% (mixed) — he voted pro-life on roughly half of TAL's scored bills, but no abortion-specific roll call was isolated, so individual sanctity-of-life cells are left null rather than guessed. Freedom Index per-bill 'Yes' marks (HB796 sovereignty, SB17 DEI ban, HB1056 gold/silver) are scored-direction artifacts, NOT confirmed yeas — SB17 in fact passed party-line with Democrats opposed — so they were not scored as deviations. No church/faith affiliation public.

0.note: TAL 2025 = 52% (mixed); no abortion-specific roll call isolated, so personhood cell left null rather than inferred.

4.note: No PP/NARAL/EMILY's List money or endorsement documented.

0.note: Openly gay; LGBTQ Victory Fund Spotlight candidate — does not affirm exclusive man-woman marriage.

1.note: Openly gay, Victory Fund-endorsed; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

2.note: Spoke on the House floor against SB14, defending gender-affirming care: it 'prevent[s] people from harming themselves.'

4.note: Victory Fund candidate vowing to kill 'bills that attack marginalized communities'; does not oppose LGBTQ-identity policy.

0.note: Voted for the budget amendment barring state funds for private-school vouchers (anti-ESA position).
No evidence.
Freedom Index HB1056 mark is unverified direction; not scored.
No paper-ballot position found.

1.note: No voter-ID vote isolated (SB1 predates his tenure).

0.note: No anti-defund/pro-police vote found.
Freedom Index HB796 sovereignty mark unverified; not scored.
No border-barrier position found.

2.note: SB4 individual vote not confirmed; no documented sanctuary-policy stance to score.
